articles.title: Iran War Live Updates: U.S. Tells Citizens to Leave Iraq After Second Attack on Embassy
articles.content: An Iran-backed militia took responsibility for an overnight attack on the U.S. Embassy in Baghdad. President Trump called on other nations to help escort oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Netanyahu Has the War He Always Wanted, but on Trump’s Terms
articles.content: Israel’s prime minister wanted regime change in Iran. But President Trump seems prepared to settle for something less.

articles.title: Mahmood Mamdani, Father of NYC Mayor, Is Writing a Book on Gaza
articles.content: At once, Mahmood Mamdani’s fame was eclipsed by his son’s. At the same time, the election of Zohran Mamdani has attracted new interest in his father’s work.

articles.title: His Film Is Spain’s Submission to the Oscars. He’s Not Sure How Spanish It Is.
articles.content: Spanish cinema has entered a new and more diverse era, film experts say. Oliver Laxe, the director of Oscar-nominated “Sirat,” embodies the shift.

articles.title: Explosion at Jewish School in Amsterdam Is 2nd Antisemitic Attack in 2 Days, Officials Say
articles.content: No one was hurt in the explosion at a Jewish school in Amsterdam, or in an earlier one at a Rotterdam synagogue.

articles.title: Is Latin America Ready to Abandon Cuba?
articles.content: Latin America’s left saw Cuba as its lodestar. Now leaders across the spectrum are hesitant to aid a nation in the Trump administration’s cross hairs.

articles.title: Cuba’s History Since the Revolution, in Photos
articles.content: The Cuban government, which has so far outlasted 13 U.S. presidents, faces its gravest challenge yet. Images from The New York Times and others record nearly seven decades of political turmoil, economic crises and small moments of ordinary life.

articles.title: BBC visits aftermath of Israeli strike on Lebanon that killed family as IDF targets Hezbollah
articles.content: A relative tells BBC those killed were civilians and not Hezbollah operatives, but the Israel Defence Forces says it was targeting "terrorist infrastructure".

articles.title: Three more Iran football team members change minds over asylum
articles.content: Four of seven members of the women's football delegation who originally sought to stay in Australia have now chosen to return home.

articles.title: 'Gruesome' war bets fuel calls for crackdown on prediction markets
articles.content: Predictions markets have hosted millions of dollars of bets related to the war in Iran.

articles.title: Hamas urges key ally Iran to halt attacks on Gulf states
articles.content: The Palestinian group also affirms Tehran's right to defend itself from "aggression" by the US and Israel.

articles.title: Cuban protesters ransack Communist office as energy crisis deepens
articles.content: Discontent is mounting over rolling blackouts and shortages of food, fuel and medicine, exacerbated by a US blockade.

articles.title: Iraq taking private plane to Mexico for World Cup play-off
articles.content: Iraq will travel to Mexico by private plane for their World Cup play-off despite calls from coach Graham Arnold to postpone the match amid the escalating US-Israeli war with Iran, the country's FA president Adnan Dirjal says.

articles.title: Treasure hunter freed from prison after refusing to turn over shipwreck gold
articles.content: Tommy Thompson spent more than a decade in prison after refusing to disclose the whereabouts of 500 missing gold coins.

articles.title: Russia exploiting Iran war, Zelensky says after fresh bombardment
articles.content: Volodymyr Zelensky cast concern over the supply of air-defence missiles after a heavy overnight attack kills at least five.

articles.title: Five arrested in Cuba after protest at local Communist party office
articles.content:
Rare action began peacefully but ‘degenerated into vandalism’ according to state-run newspaper

Five people have been arrested in Cuba for acts of “vandalism” after a small group of protesters broke into a provincial office of the Cuban Communist party and set fire to computers and furniture.

The incident, which also affected a pharmacy and another shop, took place in the town of Moron, a little more than 300 miles (500km) east of Havana.

Continue reading...

articles.title: Pentagon identifies 6 Air Force airmen killed in KC-135 refueling aircraft crash
articles.content: Six U.S. service members have been killed when their refueling aircraft "went down" in friendly airspace in western Iraq, according to U.S. Central Command.

articles.title: 2 DOGE staffers say 'no' regrets for people losing income: Depositions
articles.content: Deposition videos are providing a look at two people responsible for the largest termination of federal grants in the National Endowment for the Humanities' history.

articles.title: WATCH:  Most iconic and unexpected moments in Oscar history
articles.content: ABC News’ Will Ganss looks back at some of the most memorable moments in the Academy Awards’ nearly 100-year history.

articles.title: Iranian strikes, Hezbollah rockets make regular life in Israel ‘simply impossible’
articles.content: Attacks on northern Israel have been “constant” and “relentless”, says FRANCE 24’s Noga Tarnopolsky, reporting from Jerusalem. Iranian missiles combined with Hezbollah rocket attacks are making regular life in Israel “simply impossible”, she explains, noting that most residents of northern Israel have “simply fled”.

articles.title: UN force base in southern Lebanon comes under fire, one peacekeeper wounded
articles.content: A UN peacekeeping base near the village of Mais al-Jabal in southern Lebanon came under fire, sparking a blaze that wounded one peacekeeper. UNIFIL is investigating the incident, said spokeswoman Kandice Ardiel, noting that "the situation here in South Lebanon is precarious and volatile, not just for peacekeepers, but also for the civilians who live here and who have had to leave this area as well".

articles.title: Propaganda in 21st-century wars: WITNESS associate director Mahsa Alimardani speaks to France 24
articles.content: France 24’s Gavin Lee speaks with Mahsa Alimardani, associate director at WITNESS, about the challenges the militaries, but also the societies as wholes, face in a time of AI-generated content and misinformation spreading through social media.

articles.title: Literature in time of crisis: world-renowned writer Azar Nafisi speaks to France 24
articles.content: France 24’s Gavin Lee speaks with Azar Nafisi, Iranian-American writer, whose best-selling book "Reading Lolita in Tehran" remained on the New York Times bestseller list for over one hundred weeks. They discuss the role of literature and its subversive power in a time of war.

articles.title: US embassy attacked in Baghdad, after strikes kill Iran-backed fighters in Iraq
articles.content: The US embassy in Baghdad was hit by an attack on Saturday following strikes that killed three members of a powerful Iran-backed group in the capital, security sources said. A cloud of black smoke rose above the US diplomatic mission shortly after the sound of explosions on Saturday morning. Two security officials told AFP the embassy complex was struck by a drone.

articles.title: Israel and Lebanon are expected to hold direct talks in the coming days
articles.content: An overnight Israeli strike in southern Lebanon killed a dozen health workers at a clinic, with health authorities saying 26 paramedics have been killed since March 2. Israel and Lebanon ​are expected to hold direct talks in the coming days, their first since the start of ​the ‌Iran war that has ⁠drawn Lebanon deeper into conflict, Israeli newspaper Haaretz reported on ‌Saturday. Details by FRANCE 24 correspondent in Jerusalem, Noga Tarnopolsky.

articles.title: Israel threatens to strike ambulances in Lebanon in Hezbollah fighting
articles.content: ⁠The Israeli military warned ​on Saturday that it may strike ambulances and medical facilities which it said were being used unlawfully by Hezbollah in Lebanon, ​though it did not provide evidence for the claim. At least 26 medics and first responders have been killed in Israeli strikes since March 2 according to the Lebanese Health Ministry. The Israeli military says it ​takes precautions to try to reduce any harm to civilians. More details with FRANCE 24 Senior Reporter in Beirut, Catherine Norris-Trent.

articles.title: Why did the US military bomb the strategic Iranian island of Kharg?
articles.content: President Donald Trump said US forces “obliterated” military targets on Iran’s Kharg Island. The piece of land is the beating heart of Iran’s oil export system, strategically located in the Persian Gulf. For decades, it has been essential in connecting Iran’s vast oil reserves with the global market. Donald Trump threatened to attack its oil infrastructure if Teheran continues attacking ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Jürgen Habermas, one of Germany's leading philosophers, dies
articles.content: He was regarded as one of Germany's most influential contemporary philosophers. Jürgen Habermas has now died in the Bavarian town of Starnberg, aged 95.

articles.title: Balkan ex-presidents slam Iran war at Global Baku Forum
articles.content: Two former Balkan leaders criticised the US-Israeli war against Iran as intensifying a global crisis after Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

articles.title: Latest news bulletin | March 14th, 2026 – Evening
articles.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this March 14th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

articles.title: Iranian lawmaker says Ukraine's drone support makes it a legitimate war target
articles.content: Eleven countries have requested Kyiv’s help in countering Shahed-type drones amid the Iran war and Tehran's ongoing attacks against the Gulf states, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said last week.

articles.title: Russian strikes kill at least four people in Kyiv region as peace talks stall
articles.content: A Russian overnight attack on Ukraine's Kyiv region hit four districts, damaging residential buildings, educational institutions, enterprises and critical infrastructure, regional authorities said. The attack killed at least four people and wounded at least 15 more.

articles.title: Qatar repels overnight Iran raid as evacuations announced, officials outline supply readiness
articles.content: Missile interceptions and precautionary evacuations in Doha highlight rising regional tensions as Qatar activates emergency preparedness plans and reassures residents about supply chains.

articles.title: 'Iran must not be allowed to hold global economy hostage,' UAE minister tells Euronews
articles.content: In an interview with Euronews, UAE Minister of State Lana Nusseibeh appealed to the world that Iran’s "rogue state behaviour" must not be allowed to hold the global economy hostage.

articles.title: Latest news bulletin | March 14th, 2026 – Midday
articles.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this March 14th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

articles.title: Iran war: Trump says peace deal terms not 'good enough yet'
articles.content: The US president stopped short of outlining satisfactory peace terms. Meanwhile, Iran has continued to strike Gulf countries, threatening to widen attacks if Washington targets Iranian energy infrastructure. Follow DW.

articles.title: Cuba: Protesters attack ruling party's office amid blackouts
articles.content: Long, daily blackouts ignited by a US oil blockade have pushed Cuban residents to the edge. On Friday, Havana acknowledged talks with the US to diffuse the situation.

articles.title: Instagram accounts trivialize the Holocaust
articles.content: In Instagram posts, Nazi perpetrators are glorified, while their war crimes and role in the Holocaust are omitted. Victims' organizations call it an "attack on the dignity of the survivors."

articles.title: The BKA — 'Germany's FBI' — turns 75
articles.content: Founded in 1951, the Federal Criminal Police Office (BKA) was dominated for decades by men with Nazi pasts. Today, the BKA is a central pillar of Germany's security architecture — even on the international stage.

articles.title: US flag raised at embassy in Venezuela after 7 years
articles.content: The US embassy in Caracas has raised the Stars and Stripes for the first time after a hiatus of seven years. Washington's top diplomat said it marked a new era in relations with Venezuela.

articles.title: Belarus' Kolesnikova receives Charlemagne Prize in Germany
articles.content: Belarusian opposition figure Maria Kolesnikova was awarded the Aachen-based prize in 2022 in absentia. She has now collected it after having been released from prison in December.

articles.title: Celebrated philosopher Jürgen Habermas dies aged 96
articles.content: One of Germany's greatest postwar intellectuals, he continuously argued for a robust and inclusive democracy. His passing marks the end of an era.

articles.title: Germany's Greens: More than leftist, woke ecologists?
articles.content: Cem Özdemir won the election in Baden-Württemberg partly by abandoning many of his Green Party's core issues. His party is still debating whether this was a good move.

articles.title: Banknotes, beavers and a very British backlash
articles.content: Politicians are furious Churchill will be replaced on banknotes. The RSPCA wants rats and pigeons to feature.

articles.title: Trump urges UK and other nations to send warships to Strait of Hormuz
articles.content: The US president says he hopes China, France, Japan and South Korea will also send ships to defend the key oil shipping route.

articles.title: We're not profiteering on fuel. But my staff still face abuse
articles.content: Independent retailer Goran Raven says the higher oil price is "horrific" for him as well as his customers.

articles.title: Chancellor to offer support over rising heating oil costs
articles.content: Rachel Reeves says the Treasury is also looking at "different options" to help households most vulnerable to soaring energy bills.

articles.title: Iran war live: Trump urges allies to keep Hormuz open amid Gulf attacks
articles.content: US President Donald Trump says allies who rely on Gulf oil must help keep the Strait of Hormuz open.

articles.title: Pakistan strikes Afghan base after its president warns ‘red line’ crossed
articles.content: Islamabad hits Kandahar facility after Taliban drones strike civilian areas and military sites as conflict intensifies.

articles.title: Brazil’s jailed ex-president Bolsonaro hospitalised with lung infection
articles.content: Far-right leader, whose persistent health woes are linked to 2018 stabbing, endorsed son to run in this year's election.

articles.title: Bahrain and Saudi Arabia F1 race cancellations confirmed due to Iran war
articles.content: Bahrain and Saudi Arabia's Formula One Grands Prix races will not be held in April on safety grounds due to the war.

articles.title: Man City held at West Ham to lose ground on Premier League leaders Arsenal
articles.content: Manchester City draw 1-1 with relegation-threatened West Ham to fall nine points behind Arsenal in Premier League race.

articles.title: Trinidad and Tobago extends state of emergency for another three months
articles.content: The Caribbean nation has spent 10 of the last 14 months under an emergency status that curtails key civil liberties.

articles.title: Real Madrid beat Elche to pressure Barcelona for La Liga title
articles.content: Federico Valverde scores his fifth goal in three games as Real Madrid beat Elche 4-1 to move one point behind Barcelona.

articles.title: US-Israeli strike kills 15 at Isfahan factory, Iranian media says
articles.content: Iranian media report the deaths in central Iran as Tehran launches new missile salvoes at Israeli targets.

articles.title: Fertitta in weekend deal talks to acquire Caesars, while billionaire Carl Icahn waits in the wings
articles.content: The deal, if it gets done, would not be finalized until early April and is not expected to close until 2027.

articles.title: Iran threatens to retaliate against neighbors; Trump urges countries to assist in securing Strait of Hormuz
articles.content: President Trump said in a Truth Social post that he directed U.S. Central Command to execute a bombing raid that "obliterated" military targets on Kharg Island.

articles.title: The biggest release of emergency oil stockpiles in history was announced. Why crude may keep rising
articles.content: Oil prices have surged more than 17% since the International Energy Agency announced the largest release of crude stockpiles in its 50-year history.

articles.title: FCC chair slams broadcasters after Trump disputes reports on Iran-damaged U.S. tankers
articles.content: FCC Chair Brendan Carr blasted broadcasters shortly after President Donald Trump called reports that Iran struck five U.S. tanker planes "fake news."

articles.title: Trump fundraising pitch features U.S. soldiers killed in Iran war
articles.content: The email solicitation to benefit Trump’s PAC also offers donors “private national security briefings.”

articles.title: As New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani faces $7 billion budget hole, free parking may be history
articles.content: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani is facing a $7 billion budget hole, and the free parking NYC residents have taken for granted may be a revenue opportunity.

articles.title: Bitcoin beat the S&P 500, Nasdaq Composite and gold since the start of the Iran war
articles.content: ProShares' Simeon Hyman is highlighting a bullish bitcoin trend.

articles.title: A Paramount-Warner Bros. movie slate could rule the 2027 box office, but is it sustainable?
articles.content: Paramount CEO David Ellison has said he wants to make 30 movies a year and has franchises like Godzilla-Kong, Superman and Sonic the Hedgehog to lean on.

articles.title: More Iranian Women Soccer Players Reverse Australia Asylum Claim
articles.content: Three more members of the Iranian women’s soccer team granted asylum by Australia last week have decided to return to Iran, according to Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke.

articles.title: Key UAE Port Suspends Oil Loadings After Drone Attack, Fire
articles.content: The United Arab Emirates suspended loading operations at the key oil-trading hub of Fujairah after a drone strike and fire on Saturday, demonstrating the vulnerability of the country’s only export route while the Strait of Hormuz is blocked.

articles.title: Trump’s War Jolts Global Central Banks From Fed to ECB to BOJ
articles.content: Central banks from Washington to London to Jakarta are about to make their first assessments of economic damage after more than two weeks of conflict between the US and Iran.

articles.title: JPMorgan Makes Bold Push to Offload Huge LBO Debt
articles.content: Jamie Dimon has been warning for weeks — months, even — that the credit cycle will eventually sour. Now, his bank is about to test investors’ appetites.

articles.title: Bloomberg This Weekend 3/14/2026
articles.content: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joining the show today, AP International Correspondent Philip Crowther, Former Member of National Security Council Col. Joel Rayburn (Ret.), Axios Energy Reporter Ben Geman, International Maritime Organization Secretary General Arsenio Dominguez, Head of Petroleum Analysis at GasBuddy Patrick De Haan, Former Department of Energy Secretary Ernest Moniz, and Trane CEO Dave Regnery.  (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Gas Prices & Airfare Spike as Iran War Drags On
articles.content: Patrick De Haan, GasBuddy's Head of Petroleum Analysis, on "Bloomberg This Weekend" predicts gas prices will continue rising as demand increases this spring. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: US Faces Tough Choices in Iran War's Next Steps
articles.content: "You set things in motion but you can't control how they end up," Sir Lawrence Freedman, emeritus professor of war studies at King's College London, warns of the consequences of the US military strikes and the perilous paths ahead - on "Bloomberg This Weekend." (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Iran Oil Hub Strike Raises Fears of Further Supply Upheaval
articles.content: A major US attack on the island that exports the bulk of Iran’s crude is raising fears of more widespread supply disruptions in the region, further straining oil and gas markets already in upheaval after two weeks of war in the Middle East.

articles.title: US oil groups in line for $63bn windfall from Gulf war disruption
articles.content: American producers will be among biggest beneficiaries if crude prices average $100 a barrel this year

articles.title: Trump urges China and UK to send warships to reopen Strait of Hormuz
articles.content: President’s plea for help comes after he told US jets to bomb Iran’s Kharg Island, deepening fears of protracted oil crisis

articles.title: Why Iran could become the next Iraq
articles.content: The first Gulf war left a weakened regime in place without a political settlement — paving the way for future conflict

articles.title: Why has Trump left Iran’s nuclear stockpile untouched?
articles.content: US administration appears to have no plan to retrieve the enriched uranium after two weeks of military action

articles.title: US submarine attack brings Iran war to Sri Lanka’s door
articles.content: Attack on Iranian warship in coastal waters highlights country’s vulnerability to Middle East conflict

articles.content: Yemeni militant group is noticeably absent from Iran’s war with the US and Israel

articles.content: Deglobalisation is expensive for individual countries, but a windfall for their suppliers

articles.content: Drinks companies cut networks as rising costs and driver shortages undermine business model

articles.title: 11 stocks to harden your portfolio against Iran risk
articles.content: Focus on stocks that are stable when investors flee to safety and stock-market liquidity dries up.

articles.title: This MarketWatch portfolio of hated stocks is crushing the stock market in 2026
articles.content: Pariah Capital’s strategy is one thing that’s been winning during the Iran conflict.

articles.title: Why oil probably won’t go to $150 a barrel
articles.content: Three reasons to be skeptical of the current panic.

articles.title: The truth behind 401(k) withdrawal numbers: People aren’t being reckless — they’re desperate
articles.content: The good news is that low-income workers taking out hardship withdrawals are still saving, thanks to auto-enrollment.

articles.title: How to tackle your bucket list before it’s too late, according to the Oscar-nominated director of the film ‘Retirement Plan’
articles.content: “I’m on top of the hill with a view in both directions,” reports John Kelly, whose animated short ponders life in retirement and the meaning of time.

articles.title: Trump says U.S. struck Kharg Island, core of Iran’s oil economy
articles.content: The strikes destroyed more than 90 military targets, including missile facilities, but preserved oil infrastructure, U.S. Central Command said Saturday.

articles.title: Kids in hospital help penguins woo mates with painted pebbles
articles.content: “I always get really emotional when the pebbles go in,” said Rebecca Parr, who works for the Royal Zoological Society of Scotland.

articles.title: What Earth’s longest-lived animals can teach us about aging better
articles.content: Inside the growing scientific quest to understand what creatures with the extraordinary ability to defy the ravages of time can teach us about making human aging better.

articles.title: Trump urged uprising, but as bombs fall, Iranians are ‘too scared to move’
articles.content: Some Iranians living in Dubai say their families celebrated the death of the supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, but the war could leave Iran in a worse situation than before.

articles.title: They came to build China’s EV future. Investigators found conditions akin to ‘slavery.’
articles.content: Few Chinese laborers have spoken out about the abuses they allegedly suffered while toiling abroad. Until now.

articles.title: How Trump suspending sanctions on Russian oil helps Putin’s war effort
articles.content: The Kremlin hopes the Trump administration’s move to contain oil prices sent soaring by the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran will lead to further relief.
